snippet.title: RhinoShield SolidSuit/Solid iPhone 17 Pro Max Review – Still Good, But Not Great
snippet.description:
I’ve always liked the RhinoShield SolidSuit — it’s been one of those go-to “tough but slim” cases for years. But with the iPhone 17 version, something’s changed. The design’s still solid, but the drop data? Not so much. Let’s talk about why the new SolidSuit/SolidX feels a little… mid.

I'm on a mission to find the best iPhone 17 Cases out there. I'm planning on reviewing 50 cases in order to figure out the Top 5. For the iPhone 17's, I've updated my scoring system to include accelerometer/gyro level drop test data to figure out which cases will actually protect your iPhone. 

Get your own RhinoShield Phone Case Compatible with iPhone 17 Pro Max | SolidX - Reinforced Shock-Absorbing Protection, Minimal and Slim Design with Military Grade Drop Protection - Classic Black by using this link: https://mreh.ca/4r5uX6T
*This is an Amazon affiliate link that will bring you to the right Amazon store for your location

#ReviewerNotInfluencer #Reviews #iPhone

In this video:
0:00 Intro
0:30 Design
1:44 Handling & Usability
2:26 Protection Features
3:08 Drop Protection
4:55 Should You Get It?

RhinoShield Phone Case Compatible with iPhone 17 Pro Max | SolidX -  Review

-simple case
-one giant piece of plastic
-but it's not like your typical TPU cover
-firstly because this case is just TPE and magents
-the magsafe area has a ton of flex because it's not filled in
-the case has a slight round to the edges 
-which improves handling
-and the top and bottoms of the case are flared
-so the back doesn't scratch as easily and there's a bit more clearance for the screen
-the finish on the 17 version is better than the 16's 
-there's a subtle finish so when it's in your hand, it feels slick
-but if you clamp down, it locks in better than a slick case
-I will note that the MOD NX has a way better finish
-Rhinoshield should have added that to the SolidSuit from my perspective
-if you're a greasier person, make sure to get a lighter colored case because the dark ones are grease magnets
-even light fingernail scratches will leave a mark
-case is going to increase the weight of your iPhone by 43g
-increase the bulk of your iPhone by 65%
-for drop protection, the intial impact was around 10G's
-a hair below average which is a good thing
-the case did rotate a 10% quicker after the intial drop
-BUT the rate that this case settled was pretty good
-what make even less sense at first
-is that the total force going into the case is around 11% higher
-now the time it took for the case to stop moving after a drop was almost 40% slower

-SO the case eats the first drop well, it settles the iPhone down quickly BUT there's a lot of little bounces before it settles down

-pretty sure those extra air pockets in the case aren't helping and the floppy back definitely isn't helping at all.
-my guess anyways

-for face drops, the intial impact was average
-BUT unlike the corner drops, the settling rate was slower
-total force was about 9% better and it's probably
-because the time for the iPhone to settle was a fraction of second faster

-for the camera lump
-the solid suit has this massive right angled lip
-and this little tick
-the intial impact was higher by a bit
-settling rate was average
-and the total force was a hair higher
-settling time was much higher 

-again, the back needs to be a bit more rigid I think. Rhinoshield needs to fill in the magnet area

-now in previous years, Rhinoshield was gung-ho on their proprietary rubber 
-but now it's just TPE
-I always thought TPE was the hard to for type of plastic BUT apparently this whole thing is TPE?
-Neat
-Corners are hefty at 3.5mm thick, back is 2.7mm thick
-technically there is a set of airpockets in the case but it's hard to measure
-Rhinoshield calls this honey comb pattern shock grooves
-there's also these hollowed out sections of the case edge on the back
-not sure if that's for protection or a way to keep the case lighter

-for rigidity, well, it was terrible
-at 8mm, the pushback was 10.7N
-this is 64% floppier than the average case
-Yikes
-for magsafe strength, takes 10.8N to pull things off, 12 for push
-better than the average case by 16%
-now Rhinoshield is the OG of removable buttons
-they still feel the best but compared to other cases, it's not as tight
-there's a bit of wiggle from front to back
-which also means they pop out a bit easier

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

While testing the iPhone cameras, I found a major issue on the iPhone 17 Pro Max that no one is talking about. During low-light video recording, the 17 Pro Max shows noticeable jitters — and once you see it, you can’t unsee it. In this video, I’ve compared every camera mode of the iPhone 17 Pro Max and iPhone 16 Pro Max, including selfie, portrait, ultrawide, macro, zoom, cinematic, and 4K videos — and the results might surprise you.

From 8x optical zoom to Center Stage, Dual Record, and Cinematic 4K, you’ll see exactly where the iPhone 17 Pro Max pulls ahead — and where the 16 Pro Max still holds its ground. If you’re wondering whether it’s worth upgrading from the iPhone 16 Pro Max to the 17 Pro Max, watch this full comparison before you decide.

📊 Camera Modes Compared
 • iPhone 17 Pro Max vs 16 Pro Max Camera Zoom Test (1x / 2x / 5x / 8x / 40x)
 • Selfie Camera Test (4K@60fps) + Cinematic Selfie (4K@30fps)
 • Front Camera Comparison & Portrait Selfies
 • Rear Main Camera (24MP / 48MP full-res)
 • Ultrawide & Macro Photography
 • Video Test 4K@60 / 120 / 240fps
 • Action Mode Stability Test
 • Low-Light & Night Mode Samples (Photo + Video)
 • Dual Record Feature Demo

🧠 Key Findings
 • iPhone 17 Pro Max delivers better details, color accuracy, and dynamic range
 • 8x optical zoom & Center Stage make it more creator-friendly
 • Low-light video suffers from frame rate drops & lens flare issues
 • iPhone 16 Pro Max still performs strongly in portrait & natural tone rendering

📱 Verdict
The iPhone 17 Pro Max camera system is more refined, not revolutionary. If you already have a 16 Pro Max, this might not be a must-upgrade — unless you care about zoom and selfie improvements.

snippet.title: I'm Keeping the iPhone 17 Pro Max - Here's Why (S25 Users Will Hate This)
snippet.description:
After 60 days with both the iPhone 17 Pro Max and Samsung S25 Ultra, I'm making my final decision. A lot of you said I'd be back on iPhone... and you were right. But not for the reasons you think.

I genuinely switched to the S25 Ultra for two months to see if I could leave Apple's ecosystem. I tested both phones in real-world scenarios: camera performance at car/bike meets, video editing workflow with my MacBook, ecosystem integration, and daily reliability.

This video breaks down what the S25 Ultra does better (that zoom is insane), where the iPhone 17 Pro Max wins, and why ecosystem matters more than specs. Whether you're debating switching from Android to iPhone or choosing between these flagship phones - this will help you decide.
No BS. Just 60 days of real-world testing.

snippet.title: ALREADY AN IPHONE 17 PRO MAX NOT WORKING??? WOW
snippet.description:
iPhone 17 Pro Disassembly Video: https://youtu.be/iIMICFvL7MM

We already got an iPhone 17 Pro Max that’s not working! 😩 The customer said she woke up this morning to find her phone completely dead — no power, no charging, nothing. We tried charging it, but the power draw was stuck at 0.48A, which usually means there’s a short on the board. C’mon Apple, already?!

We decided to take the risk — removed the screen, plugged in the customer’s original battery, reconnected the original screen, and… boom, it powered right up! ⚡️

If you’re repairing an iPhone 17 Pro Max with similar symptoms, try this method first before diving deeper — it might just save you hours of troubleshooting!

snippet.title: WARNING: Apple’s clear case RUINED my iPhone 17 Pro Max!
snippet.description:
I’ve trusted Apple’s clear cases for a long time. However, with the iPhone 17 Pro Max moving to an aluminum frame, the 2025 Apple Clear Case falls short. 

In this video, I walk through design changes, grip, MagSafe performance, and a real drop that left dents and scuffs on the phone—even with the case on. You’ll see why the screen/lens lips and corner protection aren’t enough, what features I wish Apple added (better shock absorption, taller lips, reinforced corners, and a built-in stand), and the kind of case that actually makes sense for aluminum this year.

snippet.title: iPhone 17 Pro Max vs Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra - THE MOST POWERFUL PHONES IN HISTORY !
snippet.description:
The 2026 Summit War is officially underway! 👑

The ultimate mobile showdown pits Apple's pursuit of perfection against Samsung's drive for superior features. This deep dive compares the iPhone 17 Pro Max and Galaxy S26 Ultra across 5 critical categories, analyzing which flagship makes the smarter move in design, power, and camera technology.

5 CRITICAL CATEGORIES WE ANALYZE:

CAMERA DUEL: The battle of light control: Is the iPhone's Variable Aperture Lens better than the S26 Ultra's pure light gathering power via its f/1.4 Lens Upgrade?

POWER EFFICIENCY: The clash of 2nm chips! Analyzing the efficiency risks of Samsung's Exynos 2600 against Apple's TSMC-made A20 chip.

ERGONOMICS & DESIGN: Will the S26 Ultra's move to more rounded, ergonomic corners finally surpass the iPhone's boxy titanium design in comfort?

BATTERY & CHARGING: S26 Ultra's commitment to a 5,400 mAh battery and Qi2 magnetic charging as a mandatory challenge to iPhone's dominance.

PRIVACY vs AESTHETICS: Comparing the AI Privacy Display (S26 Ultra) against the sleek design of the Under-Display Face ID (iPhone 17 Pro Max).

Who will be crowned the King of 2026? Watch the full analysis!
